headline: Equifax (EFX) Is Up 6.4% After Record Q1 Boosted By Mortgage, Cloud And AI Products
summary: Equifax reported first-quarter 2026 results on April 21, with revenue rising to US$1,648.9 million and net income to US$171.5 million, alongside higher basic and diluted earnings per share from continuing operations versus a year earlier. The company’s record quarter was underpinned by strong U.S. mortgage activity, ongoing cloud and AI-enabled product innovation, and new offerings such as The Work Number Record Indicator that combine credit and employment data to streamline loan...

headline: Equifax Q1 Earnings Call Highlights
summary: Equifax (NYSE:EFX) reported what CEO Mark Begor called a “very strong” start to 2026, driven by outperformance in U.S. mortgage and continued momentum in Workforce Solutions, while keeping its full-year outlook unchanged amid heightened macro and interest-rate uncertainty tied to the Iran conflict.

headline: Equifax (NYSE:EFX) Exceeds Q1 CY2026 Expectations
summary: Credit reporting giant Equifax (NYSE:EFX) beat Wall Street’s revenue expectations in Q1 CY2026, with sales up 14.3% year on year to $1.65 billion. The company expects next quarter’s revenue to be around $1.70 billion, close to analysts’ estimates. Its non-GAAP profit of $1.86 per share was 9.7% above analysts’ consensus estimates.
